between_subjects_variables.name: anchor_condition
between_subjects_variables.description: High anchor: participants receive high numerical anchor before estimating. Low anchor: participants receive low numerical anchor before estimating.

between_subjects_variables.name: question
between_subjects_variables.description: Three estimation questions: Washington presidency year, Chicago population, Mt. Everest boiling point.

dependent_variables.name: numerical_estimate
dependent_variables.measurement: Participant's numerical estimate for the assigned question

gender_distribution.note: Gender distribution not reported in original study

washington.question_text: In what year did George Washington become the first President of the United States?

chicago.question_text: What is the population of Chicago (in millions)?

everest.question_text: At what temperature (in Fahrenheit) does water boil on Mt. Everest?

instructions.general: You are participating in a study about numerical estimation and judgment. You will be asked to estimate some factual quantities.
instructions.task: Before making your estimate, you will see a randomly generated number. This number is not related to the correct answer - it's just a reference point. Please provide your best estimate for each question.
instructions.response_format: Provide a numerical estimate in the appropriate units (year, millions, or degrees Fahrenheit)

procedure.description: Participants receive a numerical anchor value presented as randomly generated, then make a comparative judgment (higher/lower than anchor), followed by providing their absolute numerical estimate. The anchor value is actually experimentally manipulated (high vs. low) to test anchoring effects.

steps.details: Participant sees: 'A number has been randomly generated: [ANCHOR]'
Participant judges: 'Is your answer higher or lower than [ANCHOR]?'
Participant provides numerical estimate for the question

key_measures.primary: numerical_estimate (mean difference between high and low anchor conditions)

manipulation_check.description: Verify participants provided numerical responses and understood the task
questions: Did you provide a numerical estimate?
Did you see the anchor number before making your estimate?
